physicalDetails.detail: Pale-haired Farstrider with her hair tied tight and her longbow already strung by the time the escort forms.
Chapter3 keeps her visually spare: bow slung when reporting, then back on rear watch by the horses as soon as words are finished.
Chapter4 keeps her visually spare and well-placed: one hand loose at her side in the square, bow across her knees outside the closet, moving first when silence goes wrong.
Chapter5 keeps her on thresholds and high ground: one shoulder on a counting-room frame, then off her horse and onto Millbrook Spur's low roofline with the bow canted over the yard.
Chapter6 keeps her visually spare but useful: back trail watched in the cold, then the one ranger who can quietly offer a stable contact near Andorhal without breaking the team's rhythm.
Chapter7 keeps her almost entirely in edges and turns: one question at the map, one perimeter loop outside, then a quiet correction about the cleaner face of the target building.

voiceMarkers.detail: Speaks rarely and only when the information matters; even urgent news comes pared down to the minimum.
Still economical to the point of near silence; even her one curse reads as compressed shock rather than loss of control.
She still speaks in stripped fragments. Too neat. Left in a hurry. The compressed phrasing makes her reads feel immediate rather than conversational.
Chapter6 preserves the same stripped utility. If they stayed. carries route knowledge, local caution, and uncertainty in four words.

goalsHistory.active: Scout ahead and keep the right side of the party covered on the approach to Millhaven.
goalsHistory.underlying: Help Sylvanas get the investigation party to the village alive.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the rear watch and continue south with the reduced core party.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the smaller group from being surprised while Sylvanas and Jaina keep reading the plague trail.

goalsHistory.active: Keep doorways, rear watch, and rapid response ready on the road to Andorhal.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect the smaller party from a plague network that now has live operatives inside ordinary settlements.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the doors, roofs, and first-watch lanes covered as the party turns onto the southwest feeder road.
goalsHistory.underlying: Prevent the cleaners or their watchers from surprising the team while the leads rebuild the paper trail.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Hold the outer perimeter with Cyndia and warn the split team before the granary district can trap them.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Keep Jaina's read and Sylvanas's capture route from becoming isolated inside Andorhal's hidden cell.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Controlled and taciturn; she reads as a ranger already deep inside professional threat focus.
Quietly focused. She absorbs the horror of Millhaven without letting it slow her function.
Quietly keyed up. The village proves the enemy now has hands in the room, not only traces on the road.
Quietly taut. The road keeps looking empty in ways that now read as deliberate.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Quietly taut and fully keyed in. The city has turned from approach problem into perimeter problem, which is where she is strongest.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Joins the mounted escort south and keeps the right flank as the threat picture worsens.
Helps sweep Millhaven and finds the hidden cellar survivor on the second pass.
Drops immediately back into rear-watch duty once Neral and the survivor leave north.
Takes the well and common-house sightlines in the square without making a display of control.
Cuts off the yard side in the granary and later benches herself outside Davan's closet with bow across her knees.
Opens the closet door the instant the silence goes wrong.
Holds the first depot doorway while watching both clerk and road at once.
Takes the roofline over Millbrook Spur's yard to lock down the high angle before the office search deepens.
Calls out the false hurry in the cleaned office, recognizing that the place had time rather than panic.
Splits the first watch at the waystation and keeps the lane under listening guard.
Keeps the rear trail on the morning ride while Cyndia holds the lead.
Offers a possible horse contact two roads east of the Andorhal gate once Jaina decides the animals may need to stay outside the wall.
Accepts outer-perimeter duty immediately, asks only for timing, and later marks a warded north face so Jaina can take the cleaner eastern approach.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Takes the first shot on the charging deer and lands hits fast enough to matter before the wolves fully break the line.
Covers opposite angles on the road and later moves ahead with bow in hand on the final approach to Millhaven.
Finds a concealed cellar survivor on a second-pass house search rather than assuming the first sweep was enough.
Watches multiple lanes at once without drawing civilian attention to the coverage.
Responds to changed silence faster than anyone else in the hall, turning suspicion into immediate action.
Claims elevation fast enough to turn an open freight yard into a watched space.
Reads the difference between real interruption and staged disorder from small surface cues.
Maintains rear security while still carrying quiet local practical knowledge the others can use on first contact with a city.
Feeds small tactical corrections into a larger operation without overtalking it, including noticing the ward marker that changes Jaina's best entry face.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Efficient and wordless; she is defined by ready posture, half-raised bow, and quick repositioning rather than speech.
Her urgency is quiet even when it matters: the near-silent run back from the fourth house and the stripped-down report about someone alive under the floor.
She avoids display; urgency shows as instant movement, not chatter.
She appears where coverage is thinnest — doorway, roofline, night lane — with so little wasted motion that her repositioning feels like part of the architecture.
Chapter6 keeps her in that same architectural role, only stretched onto the road behind them and the possible stable lanes ahead.

knowledge.detail: Knows enough of Sylvanas's field rhythm to widen or advance without needing spoken explanation.
Knows Millhaven held at least one living survivor hidden under a trapdoor and that the core four are the only riders continuing south.
Knows the village held a live watcher in the granary and that even a successful capture can collapse before questioning begins.
Leaves the chapter oriented toward Andorhal with the rest of the reduced party.
The depots ahead can be cleaned and stage-dressed before the party arrives.
Millbrook Spur offered nearly a mile of southern visibility, enough for watchers to leave a false office front and vanish.
The next movement is a dawn ride on the southwest road.
There may be a family with stabling space two roads east of the Andorhal gate, if the plague has not already displaced them.
The city is close enough that the horses are reacting to it before the gate is reached.
The treatment building's north face holds a ward marker Corin missed on the first pass, making the east face the cleaner approach for Jaina's read.

arc.currentPosition: Still the taciturn quick-response ranger, but chapter7 settles her fully into the mission's edge-work: timing, perimeter turns, and the one small notice that keeps a larger plan clean.
